headline: Clemson seeks rhythm against surging Georgia defense
subhead: A ranked pair clash with opposite trajectories collides in Athens.

xFactor.why: Clemson's passing game must establish early rhythm after the Oregon loss, or Georgia's front seven will dominate the line of scrimmage.

keyMatchup: Clemson's offensive line versus Georgia's defensive front will determine whether the Tigers can sustain drives and control tempo in a road environment.
body: Clemson arrives in Athens riding a gut-check loss to Oregon but still holding the superior record and ranking. Georgia's upset victory over LSU last week hints at genuine defensive maturation, yet the Bulldogs remain a team still finding its identity at 2-3. The visiting Tigers need to establish early dominance in time of possession to avoid letting Georgia's defense settle into a rhythm.

The critical variable is Clemson's ability to execute on early downs. Georgia will be aggressive up front and looking to force long third-downs; if Clemson's offensive line can hold its blocks and the backfield gets consistent touches, the Tigers should control this matchup. A sloppy first half from Clemson, however, opens the door for Georgia to stay in a game where the home crowd becomes a tangible fourth-quarter advantage.
